gaps.requirement: FR-D7 breakage-canary reliability validated against each GO provider's LIVE production DOM before build — the explicit condition the ChatGPT/Gemini GO verdict rests on — including ruling the Gemini closed-shadow-DOM gate (a categorical GO→DEGRADE flip) in or out.
gaps.quote: ChatGPT and Gemini: GO, conditional on the FR-D7 breakage canary proving reliable — neither canary has been validated against a live production DOM yet ... none of the above has been provider-confirmed against current production DOM ... required before build on any provider ... the live-DOM prototype must rule this in/out per GO provider, feeding the re-tier-by-config path.
gaps.why_uncovered: GO is explicitly conditional on this validation; ENG-2689 (spike) punted it (Chrome tool not connected); ENG-2725/E5-S2 tests only MUTATED FIXTURES; ENG-2728/E6-S2 certifies AFTER adapters exist. No story owns the pre-build live-DOM verdict-confirmation nor the §15 Gemini closed-shadow-DOM categorical flip. Not in the 575-story corpus or gap-fix set.
gaps.verdict: CONFIRMED (proposed_issue corrected) — source V6hlDjecfh is canonical (doc_type technical-spec, status=canonical); §5/§6/§9/§15 quotes verified verbatim against the live wiki page. Really unticketed: grep of feature-map.txt, breakdown/*/plan.json (incl. extension), and fix-specs.json (ENG-2696..2710, none extension-scoped) show no hit; live read of ENG-2725 (E5-S2, mutated-FIXTURE canary), ENG-2719 (Gemini adapter, fixture-only, defers live coverage to E6-S2), and ENG-2728 (E6-S2 E2E, real ChatGPT/Gemini delivery but no shadow-DOM inspection, no verdict record) confirm none discharges the closed-shadow-DOM go/no-go from §15. ENG-2735 (counsel re-review, also B6) is a distinct human/legal gate and does not cover this technical DOM check either. Build work, not process — a named browser-driven test producing a machine-checkable verdict record. proposed_issue was WRONG as drafted: it framed this as blocking ENG-2717/2718/2719 (adapter build/dispatch) and invented a pre-Foundation "B0" milestone, contradicting V6hlDjecfh's own 2026-07-15 Counsel decision, which explicitly clears BUILDING the B3 adapters without this precondition and gates only SHIPPING/GA on it. Fixed in place: milestone moved to B6 (alongside sibling pre-GA gates ENG-2728/ENG-2735), Story/DoD wording and the Dependencies "Blocks" list corrected to gate ENG-2727/GA rather than the E3 adapter stories, and Context annotated with the correction.

proposed_issue.title: [extension] Live-DOM canary validation — discharge the FR-D7 GO-precondition against each GO provider's production DOM + rule the Gemini closed-shadow-DOM gate in/out
proposed_issue.milestone: B6 — Distribution, release & quality gates — store review, versioning/auto-update, E2E, a11y, FR-O4 usability
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As a **delivery-team engineer** I want **the FR-D7 breakage canary validated against the real production compose DOM of every GO provider (ChatGPT, Gemini) before default-on injection ships to a browser store — including an explicit rule-in / rule-out of the Gemini closed-shadow-DOM gate** so that **the page-level GO verdict, which is EXPLICITLY conditional on the canary proving reliable against a live DOM, is actually discharged before GA instead of assumed**. *Correction: V6hlDjecfh's 2026-07-15 Counsel decision explicitly clears BUILDING (incl. the B3 adapters, ENG-2717/2718/2719) without this precondition — only SHIPPING/GA remains gated on "the live-DOM prototype per provider including the closed-shadow-DOM go/no-go named in section 15"; this story gates GA, not the adapter build.*

**Definition of Done:** one named test/gate `TestCanaryValidatedAgainstLiveProviderDOM` (browser-driven — captures the real compose-node selector manifest for chatgpt.com + gemini.google.com, proves pre-inject probe + native-setter insert + dispatched InputEvent + read-back round-trip on each live DOM, and records a per-provider verdict GO-confirmed | DEGRADE-flip with Gemini's composer-root shadow mode explicitly recorded). *Dispatch-blocked until ENG-2690 freezes the anchor-manifest schema + verdict-record contract.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given chatgpt.com's live authenticated compose surface, When the probe + insert + read-back run, Then the enriched string round-trips byte-for-byte and the resolved selector manifest is recorded. *Assert: live read-back == intended; manifest captured.* [Source: V6hlDjecfh §9; g9vWbSYplh FR-D1/FR-D7]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given gemini.google.com's live compose root, When inspected, Then its shadow-DOM mode is recorded; if `mode:"closed"`, the provider is flipped GO→DEGRADE and the re-tier-by-config path is triggered, never left as a silent selector failure. *Assert: shadow mode ∈ {light,open,closed}; closed → DEGRADE verdict emitted.* [Source: V6hlDjecfh §15 (closed-shadow-DOM gate), §6]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given a GO provider whose live-DOM round-trip fails, When validated, Then that provider's GO is withdrawn to DEGRADE (copy/paste) and the withdrawal is recorded, not carried forward as a ship-blocking assumption. *Assert: failed round-trip → DEGRADE-flip record.* [Source: V6hlDjecfh §5 (“neither canary has been validated”), §13 AC2]
- [ ] **AC4 (no-autosubmit)** — Given any validation run, When it completes, Then no message is auto-sent and no conversation Output is read beyond the extension's own just-written text. *Assert: no submit fired; no Output read-back at scale.* [Source: V6hlDjecfh §4/§9; g9vWbSYplh FR-D5]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestCanaryValidatedAgainstLiveProviderDOM` (AC1–AC4) driving real ChatGPT + Gemini sessions.
- [ ] GREEN: `validation/live-dom` selector-capture + probe/insert/read-back harness; per-provider verdict record feeding E5-S2 fixtures + E3 adapters.
- [ ] Gemini shadow-mode gate check; emit the DEGRADE-flip re-tier record consumed by the remote tier-config channel.

## 🧠 Context

Tier: `validation/live-dom`. §5 rules ChatGPT + Gemini “GO, conditional on the FR-D7 breakage canary proving reliable — neither canary has been validated against a live production DOM yet,” and §9's Open gap flags live inspection “required before build on any provider” (the page's own, pre-counsel framing). The spike ENG-2689 explicitly punted this (Chrome tool not connected). E5-S2 (ENG-2725) tests against MUTATED FIXTURES; E6-S2 (ENG-2728) is a real live-UI E2E run but only proves ChatGPT/Gemini delivery lands — it never inspects Gemini's composer-root shadow-DOM mode and never emits a per-provider GO-confirmed/DEGRADE-flip verdict record. **Corrected gate**: V6hlDjecfh's 2026-07-15 "Counsel decision" section supersedes the pre-build framing above — BUILDING (incl. the B3 adapters) is cleared without this precondition; "the live-DOM prototype per provider including the closed-shadow-DOM go/no-go named in section 15" is named there as a standing precondition on SHIPPING, not on build. This story is therefore a pre-GA gate (parallel to ENG-2735's counsel re-review), not a pre-dispatch blocker on E3.

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the ratified extension canon sweep

## 🧪 Testing

Named DoD `TestCanaryValidatedAgainstLiveProviderDOM` (browser-driven, live provider UIs). CS §5: drive the real DOM, no mocks in the validation lane; deterministic verdict record; assert no auto-submit.

## 📏 Guidance

CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §§5/6/7; SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t` (prove-it-live not gates-green, fail-loud verdict flips, no silent GO).

## 🔗 References

- Viability verdict `V6hlDjecfh` §§5/6/9/13/15; extension PRD `QpVnEF7aEU` + addendum `5t8uxVnnA4`; PRD `g9vWbSYplh` FR-D1/FR-D5/FR-D7; ENG-2689 (spike that deferred live-DOM); OQ1 mechanism.

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2690** (anchor-manifest schema + verdict-record contract).
- [ ] Blocks: **ENG-2727** (store packaging/distribution — ChatGPT/Gemini default-on injection must not reach GA past an unvalidated DOM or an undischarged closed-shadow-DOM gate); sits alongside **ENG-2735** (counsel re-review — the parallel legal hard gate on the same B6 milestone) as the technical hard gate. Does NOT block ENG-2717/ENG-2718/ENG-2719 build/dispatch — per the Counsel decision, adapter build is already cleared. Feeds **ENG-2725** (canary fixtures seed from the captured manifest) and **ENG-2728** (E2E proves delivery lands but does not itself inspect shadow-DOM mode).

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS (“Part of ENG-NNN”, never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE on any GO carried without a live-DOM verdict.

gaps.requirement: Periodic re-verification of every target provider's ToS/AUP (OpenAI's 30-day amend right) feeding the release gate, with a HARD re-diff of Google's revised ToS effective 2026-07-30 before ship.
gaps.quote: OpenAI retains a 30-day-notice right to amend the ToU, so this determination needs periodic re-verification ... a revised Google Terms of Service takes effect 2026-07-30 ... both documents must be re-diffed before ship.
gaps.why_uncovered: E4-S2 (ENG-2722) enforces own-surface ToS-safety at delivery but never re-checks clause text over time; E6-S1 (ENG-2727) lints packaging, not provider-policy drift. Counsel ENG-2734 is only a provisional GO. No story or corpus entry owns a periodic ToS re-verification cadence or the dated Google re-diff gate.
gaps.verdict: CONFIRMED (proposed_issue accepted, minor context addition) — source V6hlDjecfh canonical; §4/§6/§7/§14 quotes (OpenAI 30-day amend right, Google's 2026-07-30 ToS revision + Prohibited-Use Policy re-diff, xAI/X dual instruments) verified verbatim. Really unticketed: grep of feature-map.txt / breakdown/*/plan.json / fix-specs.json (ENG-2696..2710) has no hit; live read of ENG-2722 (E4-S2, own-surface/session-scope firewall, not clause-text monitoring) and ENG-2727 (E6-S1, store-review packaging lint) confirm neither re-checks published ToS text over time; ENG-2734 (Done) is a one-time provisional-GO relay, and its own follow-up ENG-2735 (B6, Todo) is a one-time counsel opinion on the working build, not a recurring dated re-fetch/re-diff gate — neither names OpenAI's 30-day amend right or the Google 2026-07-30 revision. Build work: a CI/release gate (fetch+hash+diff+acknowledge), not pure process. proposed_issue is sane — correct project/milestone (B6, alongside the packaging/E2E/counsel gates), blocked_by ENG-2690 (dispatch gate) is right, body cites real ids. Added one clarifying sentence to Context distinguishing this from ENG-2735 so a reviewer doesn't wrongly treat that HUMAN gate as already covering it.

proposed_issue.title: [extension] Provider-ToS re-verification gate — periodic re-fetch/diff of each assistant's ToS/AUP + hard re-diff of Google's 2026-07-30 ToS before ship
proposed_issue.milestone: B6 — Distribution, release & quality gates — store review, versioning/auto-update, E2E, a11y, FR-O4 usability
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As a **release / compliance owner** I want **a tracked, periodic re-fetch-and-diff of every target provider's Terms of Use / AUP feeding the release gate, with a hard pre-ship re-diff of Google's revised ToS effective 2026-07-30** so that **the viability verdict does not silently rot against a provider amendment (OpenAI's 30-day amend right; Google's dated ToS change) and injection is never shipped against a clause that moved**.

**Definition of Done:** one named test/gate `TestProviderToSReverifiedBeforeShip` (CI/release — the release is blocked unless each provider's current ToS/AUP hash matches a checked-in dated baseline or a diff has been reviewed-and-acknowledged; Google's post-2026-07-30 ToS is diffed against the 2026-07-14 baseline and acknowledged). *Dispatch-blocked until ENG-2690 freezes the baseline-record + acknowledgement contract.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given each target provider (OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, xAI, X), When the release gate runs, Then the currently-published ToS/AUP is re-fetched and hashed against a dated baseline; an unreviewed drift fails the gate. *Assert: drift without acknowledgement → RED.* [Source: V6hlDjecfh §4 (OpenAI 30-day amend), §14]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given Google's ToS revision effective 2026-07-30, When shipping after that date, Then both the ToS and the Generative-AI Prohibited-Use Policy are re-diffed against the 2026-07-14 baseline and acknowledged before ship. *Assert: post-2026-07-30 ship without re-diff acknowledgement → RED.* [Source: V6hlDjecfh §6 (“both documents must be re-diffed before ship”)]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given a re-verification finds a clause that newly bars own-session injection for a GO provider, When detected, Then that provider is flipped to DEGRADE (copy/paste) loudly, never carried as GO. *Assert: hostile new clause → DEGRADE-flip + alert.* [Source: V6hlDjecfh §3 rubric, §10]
- [ ] **AC4 (cadence)** — Given no provider change, When the periodic cadence elapses, Then a re-verification still runs and records “no change” — silence is never treated as currency. *Assert: cadence run logged even on no-diff.* [Source: V6hlDjecfh §4 (“needs periodic re-verification”)]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestProviderToSReverifiedBeforeShip` (AC1–AC4).
- [ ] GREEN: `compliance/tos-baseline` dated hashes + fetch/diff job; release-gate wiring; acknowledgement record.
- [ ] DEGRADE-flip emitter shared with the live-DOM validation gate and the remote tier-config channel.

## 🧠 Context

§4 records OpenAI “retains a 30-day-notice right to amend the ToU, so this determination needs periodic re-verification”; §6 records a Google ToS revision effective 2026-07-30 (“both documents must be re-diffed before ship”); §7 records the xAI + X dual instruments. No extension story owns re-verification — E4-S2 (ENG-2722) enforces own-surface ToS-safety at delivery but never re-checks clause text over time, and E6-S1 (ENG-2727) lints packaging, not provider policy drift. Counsel ENG-2734 gave a *provisional* GO, keeping this an open compliance obligation. Distinct from **ENG-2735** (the B6 "[HUMAN] Counsel re-review against the WORKING extension" gate): ENG-2735 is a one-time legal opinion on the shipped mechanism once a build exists (blocking ENG-2727), not a recurring re-fetch/re-diff of the underlying ToS/AUP clause text against a dated baseline — it does not name OpenAI's 30-day amend right or the Google 2026-07-30 revision at all, so it does not discharge this gap's cadence obligation.

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the ratified extension canon sweep

## 🧪 Testing

Named DoD `TestProviderToSReverifiedBeforeShip` (CI/release gate). Tiers: unit (hash/diff) + integration (release-gate block on drift). CS §5: diff the real fetched documents, not a stub.

## 📏 Guidance

CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §§5/6/7; SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t` (no silent rot, fail-loud on drift, dated baselines over memory).

## 🔗 References

- Viability verdict `V6hlDjecfh` §§4/6/7/14; PRD `g9vWbSYplh` FR-D5; counsel ENG-2734 (provisional GO — develop-first); extension PRD `QpVnEF7aEU`.

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2690** (baseline-record + acknowledgement contract).
- [ ] Gates: **ENG-2727** (release / GA — no ship past unreviewed ToS drift); feeds **ENG-2722** (own-surface posture).

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS (“Part of ENG-NNN”, never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE on any ship past unreviewed ToS drift.

gaps.requirement: Ship per-provider selectors AND tier/posture config as versioned, signed, runtime-fetched remote JSON DATA (MV3-legal: data not code) so selector repairs and GO→DEGRADE re-tiers land in hours without a store review — the delivery vehicle for the re-tier-by-config path.
gaps.quote: Remote-JSON selector packs — ship selectors and per-provider tier config as versioned remote JSON DATA (MV3-legal: data, not code), patched in hours not store-review-days.
gaps.why_uncovered: §9.1 pins the manifest IN-PACKAGE (store-release-only); §15's convergent run sharpened it to a runtime remote-DATA pack to escape store latency and carry the re-tier-by-config flip. E5-S2 (ENG-2725) versions fixtures in-package; E6-S1 (ENG-2727) is the store-gated 2–5 day update channel. No story ships the runtime remote-fetch selector/tier channel. Not in the corpus.
gaps.verdict: CONFIRMED, with a dedupe flag (proposed_issue amended, not rejected) — source V6hlDjecfh canonical; §8/§9/§11/§15 quotes verified verbatim (§15's "Remote-JSON selector packs" line, §9.1's in-package pinning it sharpens). Really unticketed in the actual story corpus: grep of feature-map.txt / breakdown/*/plan.json / fix-specs.json (ENG-2696..2710) — no hit; live read of ENG-2725 (E5-S2, versioned but IN-PACKAGE fixtures, `src/adapters/fixtures`) and ENG-2727 (E6-S1, the 2-5-day STORE-gated update channel) confirm neither ships a runtime remote-fetch data channel or carries tier/posture config. Build work: a schema/signature/version-checked fetch+swap path, concretely testable. proposed_issue project/milestone/blocked_by are sane (B5, blocks ENG-2690+ENG-2725). One real flaw found and fixed in place: this exact 'remote JSON selector pack' mechanism is ALSO independently proposed (selector-manifest half only) in the sibling gap-hunt file ext-sweep/addendum.json from the same sweep run — filing both verbatim would create two near-duplicate extension tickets. Added a Dedupe-check note to Context flagging the overlap and naming this story's non-overlapping scope (tier/posture config + the re-tier-by-config flip) so a human merges rather than double-files.

proposed_issue.title: [extension] Remote-JSON selector + tier-config packs — signed, versioned data-not-code channel to repair selectors and re-tier providers in hours without a store review
proposed_issue.milestone: B5 — Degradation & breakage canaries — copy/paste fallback, selector fixtures, fail-loud, team alert
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As a **maintainer** I want **per-provider selectors AND tier/posture config shipped as versioned remote JSON DATA fetched at runtime (MV3-legal: data, not code), schema- and signature-validated** so that **a provider UI change or a GO→DEGRADE re-tier is repaired in hours by publishing a pack, not in the 2–5 store-review days a code push costs, and the re-tier-by-config path from the live-DOM gate is actually deliverable**.

**Definition of Done:** one named test `TestRemoteSelectorPackPatchesWithoutStoreReview` (integration — a published selector/tier pack with a bumped version is fetched, signature/schema-validated, and swaps the active manifest at runtime with no code change and no store submission; a provider flipped to DEGRADE via the pack routes to copy/paste). *Dispatch-blocked until ENG-2690 freezes the pack schema + signing/version contract.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given a new remote pack version, When the extension refreshes, Then it schema- and signature-validates the pack and adopts the new selectors/tier without a store release. *Assert: pack bump alone repairs resolution; invalid pack rejected, prior retained.* [Source: V6hlDjecfh §15 (“versioned remote JSON DATA… patched in hours”)]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given the live-DOM gate flips a provider GO→DEGRADE, When a pack carries that tier, Then the extension serves copy/paste for that provider from config, no code change. *Assert: tier=DEGRADE in pack → fallback path.* [Source: V6hlDjecfh §15 (re-tier-by-config), §10]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given MV3's remote-code ban, When packs are fetched, Then they carry DATA only (selectors, tiers, cadence) and never executable code. *Assert: pack loader refuses any code payload.* [Source: V6hlDjecfh §8/§11 (MV3 bans remote code), §15]
- [ ] **AC4 (no-silent-staleness)** — Given a pack fetch fails, When delivery runs, Then the last-known-good pinned pack is used and staleness is surfaced/alerted, never a silent unknown-version write. *Assert: fetch fail → pinned pack + staleness signal.* [Source: V6hlDjecfh §9 (fail-loud); g9vWbSYplh FR-D7]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestRemoteSelectorPackPatchesWithoutStoreReview` (AC1–AC4).
- [ ] GREEN: `src/packs/remote` fetch + schema-validate + signature-check + version-pin; tier-config resolver feeding E3 adapters + E5-S2 canary + E5-S1 fallback.
- [ ] Staleness/alert wiring on fetch failure; refuse-code guard for MV3 compliance.

## 🧠 Context

§9.1 originally pinned the anchor manifest “inside the extension package, updatable only via a new store release”; §15's convergent 2026-07-15 run SHARPENED this to a remote JSON pack fetched at runtime precisely to escape store-review latency and to carry the re-tier-by-config path (the closed-shadow-DOM GO→DEGRADE flip). E5-S2 (ENG-2725) versions fixtures but in-package; E6-S1 (ENG-2727) is the store-gated (2–5 day) update channel — neither ships the runtime remote-DATA channel §15 requires. **Dedupe check**: the sibling gap-hunt file `ext-sweep/addendum.json` (same sweep, source 5t8uxVnnA4, verdict also null as of this review) independently proposes an overlapping "[extension] Remote versioned selector-manifest channel" story for the SELECTOR-manifest half of this requirement (FR-BC6/BC7: locale/A-B tolerance, SLA fix path) — that proposal does not cover the TIER/POSTURE-CONFIG half (the re-tier-by-config path feeding this sweep's own Gap 1's GO→DEGRADE flip), which is this story's distinguishing scope. Whoever files these should merge into one component-registry ticket rather than shipping two near-duplicate "remote JSON pack" stories — flagged here rather than silently double-filed.

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the ratified extension canon sweep

## 🧪 Testing

Named DoD `TestRemoteSelectorPackPatchesWithoutStoreReview` (integration). Tiers: unit (schema/signature/version) + integration (runtime swap, DEGRADE-tier routing, fetch-fail pinning). CS §5: real pack fetch + validation, no stub loader.

## 📏 Guidance

CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §§4/6/7; SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t` (data over code, signed/versioned, fail-loud on staleness, MV3-legal).

## 🔗 References

- Viability verdict `V6hlDjecfh` §§8/9/11/15; addendum `5t8uxVnnA4`; PRD `g9vWbSYplh` FR-D7; extension PRD `QpVnEF7aEU`.

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2690** (pack schema + signing/version contract), **ENG-2725** (in-package fixture manifest this generalises).
- [ ] Feeds: **ENG-2717** (adapter tier resolution), **ENG-2724** (copy/paste on DEGRADE tier), **ENG-2727** (release / update posture).

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS (“Part of ENG-NNN”, never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE on any remote-code payload or silent stale-pack write.

gaps.requirement: Treat grok.com and x.com/i/grok as two DISTINCT delivery surfaces — governed by two different instruments (xAI AUP vs the stricter, controlling X ToS), both DEGRADE to copy/paste, each with its own revisit-injection trigger and its own FR-D1 'without copy/paste' withdrawal.
gaps.quote: Grok is served on two surfaces governed by two different documents ... Use of Grok on the X platform is not governed by these Terms ... x.com/i/grok the stricter of the two, controlling whenever Grok is used on X ... FR-D1's “without copy/paste” claim is explicitly withdrawn for Grok, on both the grok.com and x.com/i/grok surfaces.
gaps.why_uncovered: E3-S4 (ENG-2720) carries a single lumped “Claude + Grok posture” whose AC4 treats “Grok” as one host; it does not register x.com/i/grok as a distinct surface, does not attach the stricter-X-ToS instrument, and carries no per-surface revisit trigger — leaving x.com/i/grok an unhandled Grok host. The §11 dual-ToS jeopardy is confirmed, not hypothetical. Not in the corpus.
gaps.verdict: CONFIRMED, proposed_issue accepted as-is — source V6hlDjecfh canonical; §7/§10/§11 quotes (xAI's own "Use of Grok on the X platform is not governed by these Terms" routing clause, x.com's stricter "any means (automated or otherwise)" clause, §11's "Dual-ToS jeopardy on Grok/x.com ... confirmed, not hypothetical") verified verbatim. Really unticketed: grep of feature-map.txt / breakdown/*/plan.json / fix-specs.json (ENG-2696..2710) — no hit; live read of ENG-2720 (E3-S4) confirms its AC1/AC4 speak of "Claude + Grok" and "Grok" as one host/one posture, with no x.com/i/grok host entry, no distinct X-ToS instrument attribution, and no per-surface revisit trigger — x.com/i/grok is a genuinely unhandled surface in the corpus. Build work: host-discriminated posture derivation + fail-closed routing, concretely testable. proposed_issue is sane: project/milestone correct (B3, same milestone as the story it splits), blocked_by ENG-2690 + ENG-2720 correct (the lumped story this refines), body cites real ids (V6hlDjecfh §7/§10/§11, g9vWbSYplh FR-D3/FR-D5, ENG-2720, ENG-2724). No changes needed.

proposed_issue.title: [extension] Grok dual-surface posture — grok.com and x.com/i/grok as distinct hosts, both DEGRADE, per-surface instrument + revisit-injection tracking
proposed_issue.milestone: B3 — Per-provider delivery adapters — injection framework, ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude/Grok posture
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As a **product owner** I want **the extension to recognise grok.com and x.com/i/grok as two distinct delivery surfaces governed by two different instruments (xAI AUP vs the stricter X ToS), both DEGRADE to copy/paste, each with its own revisit-injection trigger** so that **the confirmed dual-ToS jeopardy is honoured — x.com/i/grok is never treated as just grok.com, and neither surface silently over-claims injection**.

**Definition of Done:** one named test `TestGrokDualSurfacePostureIsPerHost` (integration — on grok.com the posture is DEGRADE/copy-paste under the xAI AUP; on x.com/i/grok the posture is DEGRADE/copy-paste under the stricter X ToS; an unrecognised Grok host fails closed to copy/paste; FR-D1's “without copy/paste” is withdrawn on BOTH). *Dispatch-blocked until ENG-2690 freezes the posture matrix.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given the active tab is grok.com, When posture is queried, Then it reports DEGRADE (copy/paste) attributed to the xAI AUP surface. *Assert: host=grok.com → DEGRADE, xAI instrument.* [Source: V6hlDjecfh §7 (grok.com ruling), §10]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given the active tab is x.com/i/grok, When posture is queried, Then it reports DEGRADE (copy/paste) attributed to the stricter X ToS surface — a separately-registered host, not collapsed into grok.com. *Assert: host=x.com/i/grok → DEGRADE, X instrument, distinct record.* [Source: V6hlDjecfh §7 (x.com stricter, controlling), §11 (dual-ToS jeopardy confirmed)]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given either surface, When shown, Then FR-D1's “without copy/paste” claim is not made and honest not-yet-supported messaging is shown per surface. *Assert: neither surface renders a “without copy/paste” claim.* [Source: V6hlDjecfh §10 (FR-D1 withdrawn on both surfaces); g9vWbSYplh FR-D3]
- [ ] **AC4 (fail-closed)** — Given an unrecognised or ambiguous Grok host, When evaluated, Then posture fails closed to copy/paste, never to injection. *Assert: unknown Grok host → copy/paste.* [Source: V6hlDjecfh §3 rubric, §7]

## 🔨 Tasks
